report.summary: Folio's core loop (autosave, version history, wikilinks/backlinks, TipTap blocks, AI improve/summarize/flashcards/ask/title, mobile capture pipeline) is solid and closely tracks SPEC.md. But there is a wide gap between the researched product bar and what shipped: several must-tier organizational features are either completely absent (command palette, note templates, in-note find/replace, margin comments, columns) or wired for display only with zero input path - tags cannot be added to a note anywhere in the UI, and the tag browser API is dead code. The flagship SRS workflow only works via bulk AI generation; there is no manual select-text-to-flashcard creation. Mobile phone capture cannot chain multiple lecture photos into one session note, breaking the spec's scan-next-page requirement on the one device it targets. Search operators (tag:, notebook:, -exclude, phrases) are fully stripped by the sanitizer despite being documented. The live schema.sql has drifted from SPEC's locked schema (no note_comments, no settings, no flashcard anchor columns), structurally blocking several features. None of this shows in the green test suite because e2e/utils.ts admits tags are seeded via direct API calls since the UI mechanism is genuinely ambiguous - a clear sign the feature was never exercised end to end. Visual/dark-mode polish is strong where features exist; the gaps are almost entirely missing or shallow surface area, not visual quality.

findings.title: Tags cannot be created or edited anywhere in the UI
findings.evidence: api.ts:57,72 expose updateNote(tags) and tags(), but no component ever calls updateNote with a tags payload except NotebookPage.tsx:105 (copies tags on duplicate only). No hashtag-autocomplete extension in buildExtensions.ts, no tag editor on NotePage.tsx. e2e/utils.ts:164-168 admits tags are seeded via direct API because the UI mechanism is genuinely ambiguous.
findings.suggestion: Add a tag input on the note page or a hashtag suggestion node mirroring WikilinkExtension.ts that PATCHes tags.

findings.title: No command palette (Ctrl/Cmd+P) exists
findings.evidence: useShortcuts.ts:9-18 has no P binding. No CommandPalette component or cmdk dependency anywhere. FRONTEND.md's shortcut list never mentions it either - silently scoped out.
findings.suggestion: Build a Ctrl/Cmd+P fuzzy action finder with its own command registry, kept separate from QuickSwitcher per SPEC.md's explicit design rule.

findings.title: No manual/inline flashcard creation - SRS only works via bulk AI generation
findings.evidence: SelectionToolbar.tsx has no create-flashcard action; no Ctrl/Cmd+L binding anywhere; BrowseTab.tsx has no Add-card button. schema.sql:68-81 lacks anchor_block_id/anchor_text from SPEC's locked schema.
findings.suggestion: Add a select-text to-flashcard flow via bubble menu or Ctrl/Cmd+L, live-linked to the source block.

findings.title: Note templates do not exist - empty notebook opens a blank page, not a picker
findings.evidence: No 'template' hit anywhere in source. SlashItems.ts has no insert-template entry. NotebookPage.tsx:368-379's New-note button opens a blank note directly, contradicting SPEC.md line 332.
findings.suggestion: Add a templates table + picker, seed a lecture-note skeleton and Cornell template.

findings.title: Mobile capture cannot chain multiple photos into one session note
findings.evidence: CapturePage.tsx:97 hardcodes mode='new' every upload; captureAnother() (121-129) never carries resultNoteId forward. Desktop ImportModal.tsx:156-200 does chain, but phones are pointed at /capture, not that surface.
findings.suggestion: Give CapturePage.tsx the same append-mode chaining ImportModal.tsx already has.

findings.title: Search operators are fully stripped, not parsed
findings.evidence: search.ts:17-25 sanitizeFtsQuery() strips to alphanumerics and ANDs every token - tag:/notebook:/-exclude/phrases all no-op despite docs describing operator support. No dedicated search page exists, only the 8-result QuickSwitcher popup.
findings.suggestion: Add a pre-parser extracting operators into SQL WHERE clauses; give search its own results page.

findings.title: Tag browser pane does not exist - /api/tags is dead code
findings.evidence: tags.ts implements GET /api/tags; api.ts:72 exposes it as api.tags(); grepping web/src shows it is never called anywhere.
findings.suggestion: Build the tag browser page using the endpoint that already exists unused.

findings.title: Drag handle has no block menu - no Turn into, Duplicate, Copy link, Color
findings.evidence: FolioEditor.tsx:108-112 wraps only a static grip glyph with no click handler. ContextMenu.tsx exists and is used elsewhere but is never wired to the drag handle. No turn-into command exists anywhere.
findings.suggestion: Wire ContextMenu.tsx to the drag handle click with Turn into/Duplicate/Delete/Copy link to block/Color.

findings.title: Columns / side-by-side layout node does not exist
findings.evidence: buildExtensions.ts has no columnList/column node pair; SlashItems.ts has no columns entry. Also blocks the Cornell-template substrate.
findings.suggestion: Add a columnList/column TipTap node pair with a slash-menu entry.

findings.title: Margin comments feature is entirely unbuilt
findings.evidence: schema.sql has no note_comments table despite SPEC.md declaring it already locked/scaffolded. No comments.ts route file exists, contradicting SPEC.md section 7's API addition.
findings.suggestion: Add note_comments table, routes, a comment mark extension, and a margin thread UI.

findings.title: Dashboard is missing Week view grid, weekly review checklist, and per-notebook recall+self-test
findings.evidence: dashboard.ts:20-42 only computes a flat 14-day heatmap. There is one global continueNote card, not a per-notebook recall card with a mini self-test. No weekly-review checklist is computed anywhere.
findings.suggestion: Add a Mon-Sun week grid, per-notebook recall objects, and a weekly-review aggregation query to GET /api/dashboard.

findings.title: In-note find and replace (Ctrl/Cmd+F/+H) does not exist
findings.evidence: useShortcuts.ts binds only K/N/Shift+F/backslash; Shift+F opens the quick switcher, not an in-document find. No decoration-based find plugin exists anywhere in the editor code.
findings.suggestion: Add a ProseMirror decoration-based find/replace plugin bound to Ctrl/Cmd+F and +H.

findings.title: Unlinked-mentions section has no working Link fix
findings.evidence: NotePage.tsx:511-520 renders the same read-only NoteCard grid pattern as Backlinks - clicking a card just navigates away; there is no action that inserts a wikilink at the mention site.
findings.suggestion: Add a Link button per row that wraps the mention text in a wikilink node in the current note.

findings.title: No personal spellcheck dictionary - CS jargon gets flagged every session
findings.evidence: FolioEditor.tsx:46 enables native spellcheck, but schema.sql has no settings table at all (SPEC.md declares it already locked/scaffolded, including a spellcheck_dictionary key), and no UI lets a student add a word.
findings.suggestion: Add the settings table and a minimal add-to-dictionary action.

findings.title: Wikilink piped display text is unreachable in the UI; unresolved links look identical to valid ones
findings.evidence: WikilinkExtension.ts:117-147 never lets a user set a custom alias despite the node schema supporting it. WikilinkView.tsx never applies a missing/unresolved class to the anchor itself - only the hover popover reflects a failed lookup.
findings.suggestion: Add alias-edit support and a distinct unresolved style applied directly to the link.

findings.title: Text/background color is a single fixed highlight - no palette, no text color
findings.evidence: buildExtensions.ts configures Highlight with multicolor:false; no TextStyle/Color extension exists anywhere, despite SPEC's color-carries-meaning design language built around a 9-color palette.
findings.suggestion: Add TextStyle+Color and switch Highlight to multicolor with the fixed palette.

findings.title: Only Markdown export ships - no PDF or DOCX export
findings.evidence: export.ts implements tiptapToMarkdown only; NotePage.tsx has a single Export-.md button; no print CSS/window.print() and no docx dependency exist anywhere.
findings.suggestion: Add a print stylesheet for PDF and the docx package walking the ProseMirror JSON for DOCX, both should-tier zero/low-dep items.

findings.title: No graph view (local, per-note plus N hops)
findings.evidence: No graph-related code anywhere. The links table (schema.sql:48-53) is BFS-ready but unused beyond direct backlinks.
findings.suggestion: Add a small BFS endpoint over links plus a force-directed view, scoped per SPEC.md to avoid shipping the whole graph.

findings.title: No exam/topic coverage tracker
findings.evidence: No coverage-related code anywhere in web/src; SPEC describes student-entered topic rows with 3-state self-rating and linked flashcard due-count.
findings.suggestion: Defer if time-constrained; flag as the next Study feature after inline flashcard creation ships.

findings.title: No AI extract-action-items-to-do-blocks feature
findings.evidence: ai.ts has exactly 5 endpoints (improve/summarize/flashcards/ask/title); no action-items route or prompt exists; BACKLOG.md lists it in the MVP checklist.
findings.suggestion: Add a sixth endpoint mirroring the flashcards JSON-array pattern, rendering real TaskItem nodes on insert.

findings.title: No AI-suggested cues/summary panel after photo transcription
findings.evidence: ocrPhotoPrompt() only returns transcription instructions, not the candidate-cue JSON block SPEC.md section 6 specifies; no review/approve panel exists.
findings.suggestion: Extend the OCR prompt to also return candidate cues and add the review/approve panel.

findings.title: No math/diagram-aware capture toggle
findings.evidence: ocrPhotoPrompt() is a single fixed prompt; CapturePage.tsx/ImportModal.tsx have no math-mode toggle in their kind-switch UI.
findings.suggestion: Add a toggle swapping in a LaTeX/diagram-fidelity prompt variant, relevant given the discrete-maths use case.

findings.title: Multi-block select plus bulk action bar does not exist
findings.evidence: No NodeSelection-based multi-select plugin anywhere; only single-block or text-range selection is supported.
findings.suggestion: Lower priority than the block-menu gap since it depends on single-block actions existing first.

findings.title: Callout only supports 3 of the spec's 4 tones
findings.evidence: CalloutView.tsx:7-11 defines only info/warn/ok; SPEC.md specifies info/warning/tip/definition.
findings.suggestion: Add tip and definition tones; definition pairs naturally with the AI summarize prompt's Key-terms output.

findings.title: Nested tags render as flat literal strings
findings.evidence: NotebookPage.tsx renders each tag as one literal chip with no split-on-slash logic anywhere in web/src.
findings.suggestion: Split on slash at render time for a nested chip look, once tag-creation UI exists at all.

findings.title: No PWA manifest or service worker
findings.evidence: index.html has no manifest link, theme-color, or apple-touch-icon; web/public has no manifest.webmanifest or service worker.
findings.suggestion: Add an installable manifest for the /capture shortcut given the repeated-lecture-capture use case.

findings.title: Live schema.sql has drifted from SPEC's locked schema
findings.evidence: schema.sql has no note_comments table, no settings table, and flashcards lacks anchor_block_id/anchor_text - all three declared already locked/scaffolded in SPEC.md section 3.2.
findings.suggestion: Reconcile schema.sql with SPEC.md in one migration pass; several top findings share this root cause.

report.summary: The server passes its test suite, but the suite mostly exercises happy-path CRUD and the well-covered SM-2/version/search mechanics. It does not touch concurrency, cross-file consistency, or the AI/import pipeline's failure modes, and that's exactly where the real problems live. The single most serious issue is a genuine data-loss race: import 'append'/'improve' modes do an un-synchronized read-modify-write on a note's content, so two overlapping imports (or an import racing a live edit) silently clobber each other with no lock, version-conflict check, or queueing per note. Close behind is an architectural gap in the wikilink system: the editor already stores a resolved noteId on every wikilink node, but the server throws that away and re-resolves links by re-parsing plain-text titles on every save, so renaming a note or having two notes share a title silently breaks or misdirects backlinks, a core feature of an 'Obsidian-grade' app. There's also a second, independently-written wikilink extractor in routes/imports.ts that doesn't understand `[[Title|Alias]]` syntax, so any note with an aliased link that later goes through append/improve-import quietly loses that link. Server-side input validation is thin (zod is a dependency but is never imported): POST/PATCH /api/notes accepts literally any JSON as contentJson, including null, which round-trips into a broken note the client can't render. The AI layer sends full note content to the LLM gateway with no size guard on improve/summarize/flashcards/title (only /ask truncates), so a large note can hang for minutes across the whole model-fallback chain before failing. The SM-2 scheduler has a real bug (a card rated 'hard' from fresh can never leave the 10-minute relearn loop) and a missing ease ceiling. Dashboard/day-boundary logic uses UTC dates, which will misreport 'reviewed today' and the activity graph for a non-UTC (e.g. UK) student roughly half the year. On the access-control side, the app deliberately ships with no auth for LAN-only trust (documented, accepted), but CORS is wide open on top of that, which is a materially bigger, undocumented exposure: any website the student visits in the same browser, on any network, can read/write/delete their notes via a simple cross-origin fetch. None of this is exotic. It's the kind of thing that surfaces the first time two uploads land close together, a note gets renamed, or the app is used for a real term's worth of notes and traffic.

findings.title: Concurrent/overlapping imports on the same note silently lose data (read-modify-write race)
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/imports.ts appendMarkdownToNote (140-157) and mergeMarkdownIntoNote (159-187) each do `SELECT * FROM notes WHERE id=?` then, after (for merge) an AI call that can take up to config.ai.timeoutMs (90s default), `UPDATE notes SET content_json=?, content_text=? WHERE id=?` using content computed from the SELECT snapshot. Jobs are dispatched via `setImmediate` (line 302) with zero per-note locking in jobs.ts. If a second import (or a normal editor PATCH) targets the same noteId while the first is mid-flight, its write is based on stale content and overwrites whatever the other write added.
findings.suggestion: Serialize writes per note (e.g. an in-process mutex keyed by noteId, or a compare-and-swap using updated_at/a version column checked in the UPDATE's WHERE clause) so a losing writer detects the conflict and retries against fresh content instead of silently overwriting.

findings.title: Renaming a note silently orphans backlinks; duplicate titles resolve non-deterministically
findings.evidence: server/src/lib/links.ts: the wikilink TipTap node already carries a resolved `noteId` attribute (web/src/features/editor/WikilinkExtension.ts:49-58), but `renderText` (83-88) deliberately serializes only `[[Title]]`, and `syncLinksForNote` (27-39) re-resolves the target purely by `findByTitleStmt`: `SELECT id FROM notes WHERE lower(title)=lower(?) LIMIT 1` (line 19), no ORDER BY/tiebreak. Renaming the target note means the next save of any note that links to it re-resolves the old title to nothing and the link silently vanishes from the `links` table (backlinks lost), even though the correct noteId is still sitting unused in the referencing note's own contentJson. Two notes sharing a title resolve links to whichever one `LIMIT 1` happens to return.
findings.suggestion: Persist the noteId at link-extraction time instead of re-deriving by title text: either have syncLinksForNote read contentJson (where noteId is already known) instead of the plain-text projection, or store noteId directly in the wikilink's serialized form and only fall back to title-matching for genuinely unresolved links.

findings.title: A second, non-equivalent wikilink extractor in imports.ts drops [[Title|Alias]] links
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/imports.ts defines its own `WIKILINK_RE = /\[\[([^[\]]+)\]\]/g` (line 17) and `syncLinks` (86-101), duplicating server/src/lib/links.ts's `syncLinksForNote` but without support for the `[[Title|Display]]` alias syntax that lib/links.ts's regex explicitly handles (`/\[\[([^\[\]|]+)(?:\|[^\[\]]*)?\]\]/g`, line 4). Any note containing an aliased wikilink that is later appended-to or AI-merged via the import flow (appendMarkdownToNote/mergeMarkdownIntoNote both call the local `syncLinks`, lines 155 and 185) has that link silently deleted, because the whole `Title|Alias` string gets treated as one unmatched title.
findings.suggestion: Delete the duplicated extractor in imports.ts and call lib/links.ts's `syncLinksForNote` for both paths so there is exactly one source of truth for wikilink parsing.

findings.title: No input validation on note contentJson: arbitrary/null JSON is persisted and can brick a note
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/notes.ts: POST `/` (141-143) does `b.contentJson !== undefined ? b.contentJson : {...}` then `JSON.stringify(contentJsonObj)` with no shape check; PATCH `/:id` (189) does the same. `b.contentJson = null` passes the `!== undefined` guard, gets stored as the literal string "null", and on the next GET, `noteFull` (lib/serialize.ts:52) does `JSON.parse(row.content_json)` → returns `null` to the client, which the TipTap editor has no defined behavior for. zod is a listed dependency (server/package.json) but is never imported anywhere in server/src.
findings.suggestion: Validate incoming contentJson against a minimal TipTap-doc shape (`{type:'doc', content: Array}`) before persisting, rejecting with 400 otherwise; this is exactly the kind of check zod is already in the dependency tree for.

findings.title: reviewedToday and weekActivity use UTC day boundaries, not the student's local day
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/study.ts:159 runs `WHERE date(reviewed_at) = date('now')` (SQLite `date('now')` is UTC). server/src/routes/dashboard.ts:22-28 builds the 14-day activity window with `Date.UTC(...)` and slices ISO dates. For any non-UTC timezone (e.g. UK during BST, UTC+1), the day boundary is off by an hour window every day. Reviews done between local midnight and 1am can count toward the wrong day, and the streak/activity graph misreports accordingly.
findings.suggestion: Accept a client-supplied UTC offset (or compute local-day boundaries server-side from a configured timezone) instead of relying on SQLite's/JS's UTC-only date functions for anything framed as 'today'.

findings.title: CORS is wide open, exceeding the documented 'trusted LAN only' risk acceptance
findings.evidence: server/src/app.ts:20 calls `app.use(cors())` with no origin restriction, on top of server/src/config.ts:27 binding `0.0.0.0` by default. docs/SPEC.md explicitly accepts 'no auth, LAN-only, trusted network' as a documented risk (§8), but open CORS is a different, broader exposure than that: it means any third-party website the student's browser visits, on ANY network (not just an untrusted LAN), can issue a same-browser cross-origin fetch to the locally-running Folio server and read/write/delete every note, because the browser (not the network) is what reaches localhost/LAN-IP, and CORS is what's supposed to gate that.
findings.suggestion: Restrict CORS to the app's own known origin(s) (the Vite dev origin / the served SPA origin) instead of the default allow-all; this doesn't reopen the LAN-auth debate, it just closes a gap the LAN-trust decision didn't actually cover.

findings.title: AI improve/summarize/flashcards/title send full note content with no size guard: large notes hang or fail across the whole model chain
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/ai.ts: `/improve` (51), `/summarize` (66), `/flashcards` (94), `/title` (207) all pass `note.content_text` to `chat()` verbatim with no truncation, unlike `/ask` which slices each note to 2500 chars (line 189). `chat()` (ai/client.ts:49-61) tries every configured model in sequence, each with its own `config.ai.timeoutMs` (default 90s). A large note (near the 20MB JSON body limit, or just a long transcribed lecture) can exceed a smaller model's context window on every fallback, producing up to ~4.5 minutes of hang (or ~13 min for flashcards' 3-attempt retry loop, ai.ts:89-119) before a 502.
findings.suggestion: Apply the same truncation/chunking strategy used in /ask to the other AI routes, or chunk+summarize-then-combine for notes over a token threshold, and surface a clear 'note too long' error before making the model calls.

findings.title: SM-2 scheduler: a card rated 'hard' from fresh never leaves the relearn loop; ease has no ceiling
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/study.ts 'hard' case (104-116): when `reps===0 && interval===0`, both stay 0 and only `dueAt` moves 10 minutes out. There is no path out of that branch, so a card repeatedly rated 'hard' is due again in 10 minutes forever and never graduates to a spaced interval (server/test/study.test.ts only asserts the *first* hard rating, never a second consecutive one from that state). Separately, 'easy' (123-136) does `ease = ease + 0.15` with no upper clamp (only 'again'/'hard' clamp at MIN_EASE=1.3 as a floor), so a run of 'easy' ratings makes `interval = interval * ease * 1.3` compound without bound, producing unrealistic multi-year intervals for exactly the students who are succeeding most.
findings.suggestion: Give the fresh-card 'hard' branch an escape (e.g. increment a relearn counter and graduate after N hards), and cap `ease` at a sane ceiling (e.g. 3.0-3.5) the way most SM-2 implementations do.

findings.title: notes/notebooks DELETE hard-delete with cascading loss of version history, with no trash/undo
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/notes.ts:218-223 and server/src/routes/notebooks.ts:75-80 both issue a bare `DELETE FROM ...`; schema.sql:31 has `note_versions.note_id REFERENCES notes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`, so deleting a note (directly, or via a notebook cascade) destroys its entire version history in the same operation. The client does show a confirm dialog first (web/src/pages/NotebookPage.tsx:402, web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx:377-378), so this is disclosed, not silent. But docs/SPEC.md's own pitch is 'a Google-Docs-grade sense of safety... real version history... never feels one crash away from gone,' and one confirmed click both deletes the note and destroys the exact safety net (version history) that promise depends on.
findings.suggestion: Add a soft-delete/trash window (e.g. an `archived_at`/`deleted_at` flag with a scheduled hard-purge after N days) so the confirm dialog isn't the only line of defense against a misclick, and so version history outlives the note it protected.

findings.title: /api/import/image has no multer fileFilter, so it accepts image/svg+xml and any spoofed image/* mimetype into the public uploads dir
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/imports.ts:52: `const uploadImage = multer({ storage, limits: { fileSize: MAX_SIZE } });` has no `fileFilter`, unlike the main `upload` instance (line 51) which is gated by `fileFilter`/`ALLOWED_EXT`/`MIME_EXT`. The route's own check (`file.mimetype.startsWith('image/')`, line 331) runs *after* multer has already written the file to disk, and accepts `image/svg+xml`, which ALLOWED_EXT (line 31) explicitly excludes on the main upload path. Files are then served statically from `/uploads` (app.ts:34) with no Content-Type hardening (no `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`).
findings.suggestion: Reuse the same `fileFilter`/ALLOWED_EXT logic on the image-embed upload route so it can't accept file types the main import path deliberately blocks.

findings.title: Uploaded files are never cleaned up, so orphaned disk usage grows unboundedly
findings.evidence: The only `fs.unlink` calls in server/src/routes/imports.ts are for immediately-rejected uploads (lines 270, 332); nothing deletes files from UPLOADS_DIR when a note/attachment is deleted (attachments.note_id is `ON DELETE SET NULL`, schema.sql:57, so the file reference just goes orphaned) or when `/api/import/image` (328-336) is used. That route never even creates an `attachments` row, so a pasted-then-removed embedded image has zero DB record and lives on disk forever.
findings.suggestion: Delete the corresponding file(s) on note/attachment deletion, and either record every `/import/image` upload in `attachments` or add a periodic sweep that removes files under UPLOADS_DIR with no referencing row/content reference.

findings.title: Dashboard route recomputes word count over the entire notes corpus on every load, with no LIMIT/caching
findings.evidence: At server/src/routes/dashboard.ts:14-15, `db.prepare('SELECT content_text FROM notes WHERE archived = 0').all()` fetches every note's full text on every dashboard GET (the app's home page), then `wordCountOf` regex-splits all of it synchronously (line 15). Combined with `noteLite`/`noteFull` (lib/serialize.ts:31-61) issuing two extra per-row queries each (tags + notebook lookup) for every note returned by list endpoints, this is an N+1 pattern that scales linearly with total note/word count: fine at hundreds of notes, real at the thousands a multi-year student vault would accumulate.
findings.suggestion: Maintain a running word-count aggregate (updated incrementally on note save) instead of scanning full content_text per dashboard load, and batch the tag/notebook lookups for a page of notes into one IN(...) query instead of one query per note.

findings.title: Prepared statements are re-created per call instead of cached at module scope
findings.evidence: server/src/lib/serialize.ts:5-6: `notebookLiteStmt`/`tagsStmt` are functions that call `db.prepare(...)` fresh on every invocation (called once per note in every list/detail response) rather than being prepared once and reused; the same per-call `db.prepare()` pattern recurs throughout routes/notebooks.ts (16-17) and most other route handlers, none of which hoist a cached Statement to module scope.
findings.suggestion: Hoist `db.prepare(...)` calls to module-level constants (as db.ts's own newId/nowIso helpers imply is the intended style) so each SQL statement is compiled once per process, not once per request.

findings.title: PATCH /api/notebooks/:id accepts a non-numeric position and 500s instead of 400ing
findings.evidence: In server/src/routes/notebooks.ts:66, `b.position !== undefined ? Number(b.position) : row.position` has no `Number.isFinite` guard (unlike study.ts's `clampInt`, lines 21-25, which does guard). A request like `{"position": "abc"}` produces `NaN`, which better-sqlite3 rejects when binding, throwing synchronously and falling through to the generic 500 handler in app.ts instead of a clean validation 400.
findings.suggestion: Validate `position` with the same `Number.isFinite` pattern already used in study.ts before binding it, for consistency with the rest of the API's `{error}`+4xx contract.

findings.title: zod is installed but unused: validation is hand-rolled and inconsistent across routes
findings.evidence: server/package.json lists `zod: ^3.24.0` as a dependency, but no file under server/src imports it (verified by grep). Every route instead hand-validates with ad hoc `typeof`/`String()`/`Number()` coercion, which is how the contentJson gap (finding above) and the notebooks-position NaN gap (finding above) both slip through.
findings.suggestion: Either adopt zod schemas for request bodies (it's already a dependency) or remove it. Right now it's dead weight suggesting a validation strategy the codebase doesn't actually follow.

findings.title: extractJson's naive last-bracket scan can be fooled by trailing content containing brackets
findings.evidence: In server/src/ai/client.ts:64-76, `extractJson` finds the first `{`/`[` and the *last* matching close bracket in the whole response text via `lastIndexOf`. If a flashcards completion includes any trailing text containing `]` or `}` after the real JSON array (e.g. the model echoes a `[[Note]]`-style wikilink from the source notes, or adds commentary despite instructions), the slice extends past the actual JSON end and `JSON.parse` throws, burning one of the 3 retry attempts (ai.ts:89-119) rather than correctly locating the array's true end.
findings.suggestion: Use a proper balanced-bracket/string-aware scanner (track nesting depth and skip over quoted-string contents) instead of a single lastIndexOf, so extraction is robust to trailing prose.

findings.title: extractFromUpload has no timeout, so a pathological PDF/office file can wedge an import job forever
findings.evidence: server/src/lib/extract.ts has no AbortController/timeout around `extractText` (unpdf) or `parseOffice` (officeparser), unlike `chat()` in ai/client.ts which always bounds its network calls with `config.ai.timeoutMs`. If extraction hangs on a malformed/huge file, the job in jobs.ts stays `running` forever, never reaching 'done'/'failed', so it's also never swept (jobs.ts:36), leaking that Map entry and leaving the client's progress UI stuck with no way to cancel or retry.
findings.suggestion: Wrap extractFromUpload's parsing calls in a timeout (Promise.race against a deadline) and fail the job cleanly if it's exceeded, matching the AI call's existing pattern.

findings.title: unlinked-mentions substring match is not word-boundary aware, producing noisy suggestions for short titles
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/notes.ts:293-308: `lower(content_text) LIKE lower(?) ESCAPE '\\'` with `%title%` matches the title anywhere inside a larger word (e.g. a note titled "OS" would match "cost", "loss", "position" in any other note's text), which is common for CS module abbreviations ("OS", "AI", "ML", "DB").
findings.suggestion: Add a word-boundary check (regex-filter the LIKE candidates in JS, or use FTS5 phrase matching) so short/common titles don't flood the 'possible mentions' list with false positives.

findings.title: PATCH /api/notes/:id always writes title+content_text, forcing a full FTS reindex even on pin/archive-only edits
findings.evidence: In server/src/routes/notes.ts:200-209, the UPDATE statement's SET clause always includes `title`/`content_text` (falling back to the unchanged current values when not provided), and because schema.sql's `notes_au` trigger fires on `UPDATE OF title, content_text` (103-106) whenever those columns are *named* in the SET clause (regardless of whether the value changed), every pin/archive/move-notebook toggle triggers a full FTS delete+reinsert of that note's content.
findings.suggestion: Build the UPDATE's column list dynamically so metadata-only PATCHes (pinned/archived/notebookId) don't touch title/content_text in the SET clause at all.

findings.title: PATCH /api/notes/:id runs 4 independent statements with no db.transaction wrapper
findings.evidence: At server/src/routes/notes.ts:173-216, insertVersion (optional autosave snapshot), the notes UPDATE, setTags, and syncLinksForNote each execute as separate, un-transacted statements. A thrown exception between any of them (e.g. a future constraint addition, or an OOM) leaves content, tags, and links in a state that doesn't correspond to any single consistent save.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the whole PATCH body in a single `db.transaction(() => {...})()` the way setTags and syncLinksForNote already do individually, so a mid-request failure can't leave partial state.

findings.title: recursive_triggers is never explicitly set, so FTS cascade-delete sync relies on an implicit SQLite default
findings.evidence: server/src/db.ts:9-11 sets `journal_mode`/`foreign_keys` explicitly but never sets `recursive_triggers`. Notebook deletion cascades to notes via schema.sql's `ON DELETE CASCADE` (line 17), and whether the notes_fts sync trigger (`notes_ad`) fires for those cascade-deleted rows is governed by that pragma's default, which has changed across SQLite versions.
findings.suggestion: Set `db.pragma('recursive_triggers = ON')` explicitly next to the other two pragmas so FTS sync on cascading deletes doesn't depend on whatever SQLite build better-sqlite3 happens to bundle.

findings.title: Restoring a version or merging an Import into the OPEN note never refreshes the live editor, so the next autosave silently reverts the change
findings.evidence: NotePage.tsx:101-108 keys NoteWorkspace on `state.note.id`, which is UNCHANGED for both scenarios below, so React never remounts it and its internal `note`/`pendingRef` stay pinned to the pre-change content (per FolioEditor.tsx:2-3, content is 'seed-only', only re-read on remount). Scenario A, History restore: HistoryPanel.tsx doRestore() (82-95) calls api.restore then onRestored()->onReload() (NotePage.tsx:106,526), which only refetches NotePage's own `state`, never touched by the mounted NoteWorkspace. Scenario B, Import 'Improve & merge' into the currently-open note: ImportModal.tsx handleOpenNote() (209-213) does `navigate('/note/'+resultNoteId)` where resultNoteId===note.id, i.e. navigating to the SAME path, and NotePage's load effect (32-51) has `noteId` unchanged in its dep array so it never refires. In both cases the toast says 'Note restored'/'Note ready' but the visible editor is stale; the moment the user types anything, handleDocChange->capturePending()->autosave.schedule() (NotePage.tsx:213-221) captures the STALE pre-change doc plus the new keystroke and PATCHes it 800ms later, overwriting the just-restored/just-imported server content with the old version.
findings.suggestion: After restore/import-merge into the open note, force a real resync: either bump a `version`/`reloadKey` state that is included in NoteWorkspace's key (so it remounts and FolioEditor re-seeds), or explicitly call `editor.commands.setContent(freshContentJson)` + reset pendingRef/note state from the server response before allowing further edits.

findings.title: A failed autosave PATCH clears the dirty flag before the save is confirmed: navigating away after an error permanently drops the edit
findings.evidence: useAutosave.ts:31-54. `flush()` sets `dirtyRef.current = false` at line 36 BEFORE `await api.updateNote(...)` even starts (line 40). If the request fails, the catch block (45-47) only sets status/error and never restores dirtyRef. The unmount-flush guard (92-97: `if (dirtyRef.current) void flush();`) and the beforeunload guard (71-89: `if (!dirtyRef.current) return;`) both gate on dirtyRef, which is already false. So: type -> save fails (flaky wifi / server hiccup, a totally plausible weekly event for a local-first app) -> user doesn't notice the small 'Save failed' chip and navigates to another note or closes the tab -> the edit is gone with no retry path except manually clicking Retry before leaving.
findings.suggestion: Only clear `dirtyRef.current` inside the try block after the PATCH resolves successfully (or restore it to true in the catch block), so both the unmount cleanup and beforeunload handlers will actually retry a failed save.

findings.title: NotePage's note-load effect has no race guard, so rapid navigation lets a stale response overwrite the current note with a different note's content (the known caret-jump bug)
findings.evidence: NotePage.tsx:32-51. `load(id)` fires `api.note(id).then(({note,backlinks}) => setState({note,backlinks}))` with no cancellation token, no AbortController, and no check that `id` still matches the current `noteId` param when the promise resolves. Navigating A->B->A quickly (e.g. two wikilink/quick-switcher clicks before the first request returns) can let the stale request for A resolve LAST and overwrite `state.note` after B (or a second visit to A) is already showing. Since `key={state.note.id}` (line 103) is a plain string, this either silently swaps the editor's entire visible content to the stale note while the URL bar still shows the other note, or (when ids coincide) leaves `state` pointing at a note whose id no longer matches the just-mounted NoteWorkspace's internal `note.id`, so subsequent autosave PATCHes target the wrong note. The remount also creates a brand-new TipTap editor instance with no preserved selection, which is what actually produces the reported 'caret snaps to document start mid-typing' symptom.
findings.suggestion: Add a request-id or `cancelled` flag inside the effect, matching the pattern already used correctly in ImportModal.tsx:75-100 and QuickSwitcher.tsx:59-76: `useEffect(() => { if (!noteId) return; let cancelled=false; api.note(noteId).then(r => { if(!cancelled){...} }); return () => { cancelled = true; }; }, [noteId])`.

findings.title: useAutosave.flush() drops the newest edits when a save is already in-flight instead of queuing a follow-up, so the 'Saved' chip can lie
findings.evidence: useAutosave.ts:31-54, specifically line 33: `if (flushingRef.current) return flushingRef.current;`. If a debounce timer fires flush() while a previous PATCH is still in flight (slow network), the second call returns the FIRST promise verbatim: it never re-reads `getPayloadRef.current()` for the newer content, never resets `dirtyRef`, and never schedules a follow-up timer. If the user then stops typing (no further schedule() call), the newest keystrokes sit in `pendingRef` with `dirtyRef.current` still true but no timer pending. The UI shows 'Saved · just now' (from the first PATCH's onSaved callback) while the server actually only has the OLDER payload. Recovery only happens via another distinct trigger (further typing, blur, unmount, Ctrl+S, or a page close that fires beforeunload).
findings.suggestion: Chain instead of short-circuiting: `if (flushingRef.current) return flushingRef.current.then(() => (dirtyRef.current ? flush() : undefined));` so a save that arrives mid-flight is queued rather than silently discarded.

findings.title: Ctrl+N / sidebar '+' / Quick Switcher 'Create note' silently file the new note into notebooks[0], not the notebook you're reading, whenever you're not literally on /notebook/:id
findings.evidence: main.tsx:20-24 defines `note/:noteId` as a SIBLING of `notebook/:notebookId`, not nested under it, so `useParams<{notebookId}>()` in App.tsx (line 51) is undefined on `/note/:id`, `/study`, `/ask`, and `/`. App.tsx handleNewNote (66-78) and QuickSwitcher.tsx createFromQuery (96-112) both do `const notebookId = params.notebookId (or currentNotebookId) || notebooks[0]?.id;`. So pressing Ctrl+N or the sidebar '+' button while READING A NOTE (the single most natural moment to hit 'new note') creates the note in whichever notebook happens to be first by position, not the one you were just working in. There is no toast/indicator telling the user which notebook it landed in.
findings.suggestion: Track 'current notebook' from the open note too (e.g. NotePage could publish `note.notebookId` to a small shared ref/context that App.tsx reads, falling back to notebooks[0] only when there is truly no context), or surface which notebook a note lands in via a toast.

findings.title: NotebooksContext's optimistic rollback on a failed update/delete reapplies the already-mutated state, so failures never actually revert
findings.evidence: NotebooksContext.tsx:29-33 refreshes `snapshotRef.current = notebooks` in a `useEffect` keyed on `notebooks`. `updateNotebook`/`deleteNotebook` (58-81) optimistically call `setNotebooks(...)` first, then on failure roll back via `setNotebooks(snapshotRef.current)`. Because the effect fires right after the optimistic setNotebooks commits (well before any real network round trip resolves), by the time the catch block runs, `snapshotRef.current` already equals the FAILED optimistic state, and the 'rollback' is a no-op. Concretely: delete a notebook, the DELETE request fails (server down, network blip) -> the notebook vanishes from the sidebar permanently (even though it was never actually deleted server-side) until a full reload; a failed rename/color/archive similarly sticks in the UI despite the toast saying it failed.
findings.suggestion: Capture the pre-mutation snapshot synchronously at the top of `updateNotebook`/`deleteNotebook` (e.g. `const prev = notebooks;` read from the closure or a ref updated via a functional setState, not from a reactive effect) and roll back to that captured value, not to a ref that races the optimistic update.

findings.title: Modal's focus trap re-runs on every parent re-render and forcibly steals focus back to the first control: ImportModal's 800ms job-poll makes this fire repeatedly during an active import
findings.evidence: Modal.tsx:25 `useEffect(..., [open, onClose])`: the escape/tab-trap effect depends on `onClose`. Every caller (NotePage.tsx ImportModal/AiPreviewModal `onClose={() => setImportOpen(false)}`, App.tsx ImportModalHost `onClose={() => setState(s=>({...s,open:false}))}`, ConfirmDialog.tsx `onClose={onCancel}` passed inline everywhere) supplies a brand-new function each render, so the effect tears down and re-runs on every parent re-render, and its setup unconditionally calls `(toFocus ?? panelRef.current)?.focus()` (line 32). Concretely: while ImportModal is in the 'running' phase, useImportJob.ts polls every 800ms (line 47 `POLL_MS=800`) and calls `setJob(latest)`, re-rendering ImportModal and recreating `runningOnClose` (ImportModal.tsx ~216) every time. Roughly once a second, focus is yanked back to the first focusable element in the dialog regardless of where the user (especially a keyboard/screen-reader user) had just tabbed to (e.g. away from the eventual Cancel/Retry button).
findings.suggestion: Store `onClose` in a ref inside Modal and only depend the effect on `[open]`; read `onCloseRef.current` inside the keydown handler instead of closing over the prop directly.

findings.title: Selection AI edit ('Replace selection'/'Insert below') applies at stale ProseMirror positions captured before the async round trip, with nothing blocking further edits meanwhile
findings.evidence: SelectionToolbar.tsx:52-66 `runAi()` captures `{from,to}` from the CURRENT selection, fires `api.aiImprove`, and only disables the '✨ AI' trigger button (`aiLoading`), while the rest of the editor stays fully editable. When the result comes back, applying it (142-158) does `editor.chain().focus().insertContentAt(aiResult.range, html).run()` using the ORIGINAL, un-remapped `{from,to}`. If the user edits the document anywhere else while the request is in flight (adds/removes text before that range, or deletes the node the range pointed into), the stored positions no longer correspond to the intended text, so the AI result can be inserted at the wrong location, split mid-node, or throw a ProseMirror range error.
findings.suggestion: Track the selection with a `Mapping`/set a bookmark (e.g. `editor.state.doc.resolve` positions via a mapped `Decoration`/`Selection` bookmark, or simpler: record the transaction count at request start and re-map `{from,to}` through every intervening transaction's `tr.mapping` before applying), or lock the editor read-only for that request the way `aiBusy` does at the note level in NotePage.

findings.title: Mobile nav drawer is only visually off-canvas via CSS transform: closed-state controls stay fully tabbable, and the open drawer has no focus trap or Escape-to-close
findings.evidence: shell.css:95-113 hides the closed mobile sidebar purely with `transform: translateX(-100%)` (no `display:none`/`visibility:hidden`/`inert`), so all of the sidebar's interactive elements (search button, Home/Study/Ask nav links, every notebook row + its emoji picker + '...' context menu, new-notebook form, theme toggle, phone-capture button) remain in the DOM and in the tab order even when the drawer is closed on a <900px viewport. App.tsx's `mobileOpen` is only toggled by clicking the hamburger button or the scrim (`onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}`, line 106). There is no Escape-key handler and no focus trap (unlike the dedicated one in Modal.tsx) once it IS open, so keyboard focus can leave the drawer into the scrim-covered main content behind it.
findings.suggestion: Add `inert` (or `aria-hidden` + `tabIndex=-1` sweep) to `.app-sidebar-wrap` when `data-mobile-open` is false, and reuse Modal's focus-trap/Escape pattern (or wrap the mobile drawer in `<Modal>`) while it's open.

findings.title: Sidebar and Notebook-page note counts go stale after create/duplicate/move/archive/delete, because nothing calls NotebooksContext.reload()
findings.evidence: Sidebar.tsx:289 renders `{nb.noteCount}` from `useNotebooks()`; NotebookPage.tsx:24,268 reads the same `notebook.noteCount` for its header ('N notes'). But none of: App.tsx handleNewNote (66-78), NotebookPage.tsx createNewNote (73-81), duplicateNote (97-112, which DOES call the page-local `load()` but not the notebooks context), moveNote (114-123), setArchived (125-133), or confirmDeleteNote (135-149) ever call `useNotebooks().reload()`. So immediately after creating/deleting/moving a note, the note LIST on the page updates (local state) but the notebook's count badge in the sidebar (and the 'N notes' text at the top of that very same page) keeps showing the pre-mutation number until an unrelated notebook edit or a full reload happens.
findings.suggestion: Call `reload()` from `useNotebooks()` (or have the mutating endpoints return updated counts and merge them) after any note create/delete/move/archive/duplicate.

findings.title: Wikilink 'Create note: X' command chain can run against a destroyed editor after cross-note navigation
findings.evidence: WikilinkExtension.ts:126-139 fires `api.createNote(...).then(({note}) => editor.chain().focus().insertContentAt(range, {...}).run())` with no `editor.isDestroyed` check. Because FolioEditor fully remounts per note (key={note.id}), if the user triggers '[[New Note' -> Enter and then immediately clicks away to another note before the create-note request resolves, the callback fires against an editor instance that React has already torn down.
findings.suggestion: Guard both the success and error branches with `if (editor.isDestroyed) return;` before calling any editor command.

findings.title: SelectionToolbar's '✨ AI' instruction dropdown has no keyboard dismissal and no click-outside handling
findings.evidence: In SelectionToolbar.tsx:105-113, `{aiOpen && <div className="folio-ai-dropdown" onMouseLeave={() => setAiOpen(false)}>...}` is the only close path. There's no `onKeyDown` Escape handler and no outside-click listener (unlike ContextMenu.tsx:74-101 or DropdownButton.tsx:15-29 which both wire Escape + outside-mousedown). A keyboard-only user who opens it (or any user whose pointer doesn't cleanly leave the small popover) has no way to dismiss it short of choosing one of the AI actions.
findings.suggestion: Reuse the existing `useFloatingPanel`/DropdownButton pattern (Escape + outside-mousedown) instead of relying on `onMouseLeave`.

report.summary: Folio's web client is well-built in several places that are easy to get wrong: the QuickSwitcher debounce race is correctly guarded with a request-id (QuickSwitcher.tsx:59-76), ImportModal's notebook/target-note fetches and useImportJob's poll loop both use proper `cancelled` flags, tiptap's own Suggestion plugin (verified against node_modules source) already aborts stale wikilink-search requests, and every markdown→HTML sink I found (AskPage, AiPreviewModal) is DOMPurify'd while the search-snippet sink avoids dangerouslySetInnerHTML entirely. That said, the autosave/editor-sync layer (the actual core of a note app) has real, demonstrable correctness gaps that add up to a genuine data-integrity risk: a failed PATCH clears the dirty flag before success is confirmed (so a save error + navigate-away permanently drops the edit), a concurrent flush() silently swallows newer keystrokes instead of queuing them, and NotePage's note-load effect has no race guard at all, which is very likely the root cause of the reported 'caret snaps to start' bug (a stale response can swap the whole editor to a different note's content while the URL still shows the current one). Separately, restoring a note version or merging an import into the currently-open note never actually refreshes the live editor, so the very next autosave silently reverts the restore/import. The UI says 'success' while quietly undoing itself. Outside the editor, NotebooksContext's optimistic-update rollback is broken (a failed delete looks permanent), the single most common note-creation path (Ctrl+N / sidebar +) silently files notes into the wrong notebook whenever you're not literally on a /notebook/:id route, and Modal's focus-trap effect re-steals focus on every parent re-render, concretely visible as ImportModal yanking focus back roughly once a second during an active import. The mobile nav drawer is also only CSS-hidden (transform, not inert/display), so it's fully tabbable while 'closed' with no focus trap when open. None of this is generic advice: each finding traces to a specific effect, ref, or route definition and has a concrete, plausible trigger a real user would hit.</summary>

Folio's desktop light-mode craft is genuinely close to the Notion/Linear bar: the editor, slash menu, wikilink menu, history drawer, and study flip-card all show real design intent, consistent 8px-radius/border-not-shadow language, and correct dark-theme token usage (I specifically checked 01↔02, 04↔09, 03↔18 for parity and found none of the dark-mode regressions I initially suspected, since tokens.css is applied correctly throughout). But it falls short of premium on three fronts once you look past the happy-path desktop shots. First, mobile is not evenly designed: the dashboard (the actual landing page) overflows horizontally and slices card text mid-word at phone widths, directly violating the app's own '390px width' mandate. This is the single worst thing in the pack and should be fixed before anything else. Second, there's a real, computable WCAG contrast failure (--ink-3, ~3.0–3.4:1) baked into the token system that quietly under-contrasts every timestamp and meta label app-wide in both themes. Third, the app doesn't consistently use its own icon system. Icon.tsx explicitly reserves emoji for content and vector icons for chrome, but the editor's Pin/AI buttons, History cause icons, and Import kind tabs all break that rule with raw OS emoji, which is exactly the kind of inconsistency that reads as 'built by different people on different days' rather than one considered product. On top of that, a genuine (if narrower) CSS bug makes the quick-switcher's full-text search results render with overlapping/garbled text, and the selection bubble-menu has no flip fallback so it can sit directly on top of the text you're trying to format. None of this is catastrophic, but together it's the gap between 'passes tests' and 'ships at Notion quality.'

findings.title: Dashboard grid overflows horizontally on mobile: the home screen is unreadable
findings.evidence: 16-dashboard-mobile.png: the hero card title/snippet and the 'Recent lessons' note-card grid are sliced mid-word at the screenshot's right edge ('Trac[king]', 'desc[ribes]') rather than wrapping, the signature of true page-level horizontal overflow, not a text-wrap failure. web/src/styles/pages.css:113-117 `.note-grid{grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(230px,1fr))}` has no override at the file's own 980px/640px/520px breakpoints, so a 2-column grid (2×230px+12px gap ≈ 472px) is forced even when the usable width is well under that. That directly contradicts docs/FRONTEND.md's 'must be fully usable at 390px width.'
findings.suggestion: Add `@media (max-width: 600px){.note-grid{grid-template-columns:1fr}}`, audit `.dash__hero-title`/`.dash__hero-snippet` for the same issue, and re-capture at a literal 390px viewport before signoff.

findings.title: Quick switcher full-text results render garbled, overlapping text
findings.evidence: 10-quick-switcher.png: rows under 'FULL-TEXT MATCHES' show visibly colliding text fragments instead of a clean two-line title+snippet. web/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx:210-215 renders `.folio-qs__row-title` and `.folio-qs__row-snippet` as plain `<span>` elements (default `display:inline`); components.css:668-682 puts `white-space:nowrap; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis` on both, which has no effect on inline (non-block) elements per spec, so the untruncated text overflows and collides with adjacent content instead of eliding with '…'.
findings.suggestion: Set `display:block` on `.folio-qs__row-title` / `.folio-qs__row-snippet` (or make `.folio-qs__row-main` a column flex container) so the nowrap+ellipsis rules actually take effect.

findings.title: --ink-3 fails WCAG AA contrast in both themes, so every timestamp/meta label is under-contrast
findings.evidence: Computed via WCAG relative luminance: light `--ink-3:#8b949e` (tokens.css:13) on `--bg:#fff` = 3.07:1; dark `--ink-3:#6e6e6b` (tokens.css:74) on `--bg:#191919` = 3.43:1. Both sit well below the 4.5:1 AA minimum for the 11-12.5px text this token drives everywhere: note-card meta lines (components.css:471-478, e.g. '3h ago · 301 words'), notebook-row counts, breadcrumbs, footer hints. For comparison `--ink-2` hits 6.4:1/6.9:1 and is fine.
findings.suggestion: Nudge --ink-3 (e.g. light ~#6b7280, dark ~#8a8a86) to clear 4.5:1 without disturbing the intended visual hierarchy, or restrict it to 14px+/decorative uses only.

findings.title: Interactive chrome mixes the app's own vector icon set with raw OS emoji, against the codebase's own rule
findings.evidence: Icon.tsx:1-4's own comment states emoji are 'for content identity... these [vector icons] are for interactive chrome only,' and the set already includes `pin`/`pin-filled`/`sparkles`. NoteCard.tsx:46 correctly uses `<Icon name="pin-filled"/>`, but NotePage.tsx:381 renders the editor's Pin toggle as raw `{note.pinned?'📌':'📍'}`, the AI button (NotePage.tsx:386-388, SelectionToolbar.tsx:103) uses '✨' instead of the existing `sparkles` icon, and HistoryPanel.tsx:14 / import kind tabs use ✍️📌✨⏪📥 / 📷📑📝 emoji throughout, all OS-font-dependent and visually heavier than the 1.8px-stroke SVGs used in the sidebar and search.
findings.suggestion: Route the action-bar Pin, AI, History-cause, and Import-kind glyphs through Icon.tsx (extending it with a couple of new entries where missing) so chrome renders identically across OS/browser and matches the vector look already established in the sidebar.

findings.title: Selection bubble menu renders on top of the very heading it's editing
findings.evidence: 07-editor-selection-toolbar.png: selecting from partway through the H1 into the first paragraph places the black bubble menu directly over the H1 line, hiding its middle letters ('Big-O N[…]lexity Analysis'). SelectionToolbar.tsx:73 hardcodes `options={{placement:'top', offset:8}}` with no flip/fallback, and the ~15-20px gap between the 40px title and the first H1 isn't enough room for an 8px-offset 'top' menu to actually clear the selection.
findings.suggestion: Let the BubbleMenu's positioning middleware flip to 'bottom' when there's no room above (drop the hardcoded placement or add a flip strategy) so the toolbar never occludes the text being formatted.

findings.title: Mobile capture's notebook picker hides most notebooks with zero scroll affordance
findings.evidence: 15-capture-mobile.png shows only 'Algorithms & Data Structures' and 'Databases' chips, with a third chip hard-clipped at the screen edge. Operating Systems / Software Engineering / Personal are invisible and undiscoverable. CapturePage.css:75-86 `.cp-notebooks{overflow-x:auto; scrollbar-width:none}` hides the scrollbar with no fade mask or chevron cue.
findings.suggestion: Add an edge fade (`mask-image: linear-gradient(...)`) or trailing chevron so users on this phone-first, purpose-built page know more notebooks are a swipe away. Right now it reads as squeezed rather than designed.

findings.title: Study 'answer' state doesn't visibly show an answer in the captured screenshot
findings.evidence: 12-study-answer.png is pixel-identical to the question state (still shows 'What is the main drawback…' + 'Show answer') except the card border is skewed/foreshortened. StudyPage.css:239-244 drives the reveal with a 380ms `rotateX(180deg)` 3D flip (`perspective:1600px`), so the skew matches a screenshot taken mid-transition rather than the settled back face.
findings.suggestion: Have the capture script wait for `transitionend` (or >400ms) before snapping 'answer' states, and manually click through Show Answer → rating once. This pack doesn't actually prove the answer face looks right.

findings.title: Mobile hamburger drawer's nav text is left-clipped in the captured open state
findings.evidence: 17-mobile-drawer.png: every sidebar line is missing its first 1-2 characters ('olio' for Folio, '…rch' for Search, '…OOKS' for NOTEBOOKS, '…gorithms & Data Structures'). shell.css:100-109 slides the drawer via `transform:translateX` over 220ms and resolves cleanly to `translateX(0)` at rest, so this looks like the same capture-before-settle issue as the study flip above rather than a shipped layout bug.
findings.suggestion: Re-verify live with the animation allowed to finish; if the clipping persists after settling, the mobile nav is unreadable for ~220ms on every open, which would be a much bigger deal.

findings.title: Nearly every note body repeats its own title as an H1 right below the title field
findings.evidence: 04, 05, 08, 09, 18: 'Big-O Notation & Complexity Analysis' (and Testing Pyramid, Agile & Scrum Essentials, etc.) appears as the 40px title AND again, nearly verbatim, as the first H1 in the body, with minimal vertical rhythm separating the two. It reads as one duplicated header.
findings.suggestion: Have the import/AI-generation pipeline drop a leading H1 that duplicates the title (or auto-strip it on import), and/or give the title→body transition more breathing room.

findings.title: Autosave status is blank on first paint instead of showing a settled 'Saved' state
findings.evidence: 04-editor-note.png and 09-editor-dark.png show empty space in the action bar's top-right slot where 05-08 show 'Saved · just now': the indicator only appears after the first in-session edit, so a freshly opened already-saved note looks unfinished.
findings.suggestion: Initialize the indicator from the note's `updatedAt` on load (e.g. 'Saved · 2h ago') instead of leaving it blank until the first debounced save fires.

findings.title: Ask page loads with the sidebar notebook list stuck on skeleton and only one filter chip
findings.evidence: 13-ask-page.png: the sidebar shows 4 gray shimmer bars under NOTEBOOKS instead of the 5 real notebooks visible on every other page's screenshot, and only a static 'All notebooks' chip renders. The spec's per-notebook filter chips never appear.
findings.suggestion: Confirm notebooks are fetched once at the app-shell level rather than per-route so the sidebar and Ask's filter chips hydrate together; verify manually since Ask is a core route.

findings.title: Pin icon uses a map-pin metaphor instead of a thumbtack
findings.evidence: NotePage.tsx:381 and the notebook-page list rows use 📍 (round location pin) for the un-pinned/'click to pin' affordance and 📌 (thumbtack) for pinned, while Icon.tsx already ships a purpose-built `pin`/`pin-filled` outline pair without the 'map location' connotation.
findings.suggestion: Use Icon.tsx's `pin`/`pin-filled` consistently (also resolves the icon-system finding above) instead of two mismatched emoji metaphors.

findings.title: Hero card snippet has no line-clamp, so long notes could produce an oversized hero
findings.evidence: pages.css:73-79 `.dash__hero-snippet{max-width:640px}` constrains width but not line count: there is no `-webkit-line-clamp`, even though the codebase already ships a `.folio-clamp-2` utility (base.css:145-150) for exactly this.
findings.suggestion: Apply `.folio-clamp-2` (or equivalent) to `.dash__hero-snippet` so the hero's height stays consistent regardless of note length.

findings.title: Import-kind and history 'cause' icons introduce uncontrolled multi-hue color into an otherwise single-accent palette
findings.evidence: docs/FRONTEND.md's direction reserves color for a single --accent plus notebook dots/accents ('never for large fills'); the camera📷/document📑/pencil📝/writing-hand✍️/pushpin📌 emoji in 14-import-modal.png and 08-editor-history-panel.png bring in uncontrolled full-color glyphs next to an otherwise monochrome, currentColor-driven UI.
findings.suggestion: Folds into the icon-system finding above. Once chrome glyphs move to the SVG Icon set (currentColor), this resolves itself.

report.summary: Folio's live-typing editor (slash menu, code blocks, KaTeX, tables, wikilink autocomplete) is genuinely well built and scenario 2 (fast lecture typing) is well served. Everything downstream of that falls well short of a premium bar: the three import pipelines that are supposed to be the product's headline value ("photograph a page, get an editable transcription"), the dashboard's promised "5-minutes-before-lecture" recall, exam-week study filtering, and the promised search-operator syntax. Several of these are outright broken rather than merely rough. Concretely, by lens scenario: (1) 5-min-before-lecture recall FAILS: the dashboard has no per-notebook "last time in X" card, only one global most-recent-note plus a flat cross-notebook feed (and that one note is shown twice in a row, back-to-back). (2) live editing is solid. (3) the after-lecture phone-capture flow FAILS on both explicit lens probes. Using it twice in a row fragments one lecture into separate disconnected notes (mode is hardcoded to "new" with no page-chaining, unlike the desktop import modal which does this correctly), and there is no way, anywhere in the app, to see the original photo again to check a mistranscribed word or diagram against it (the attachments table is written but never read back by any route or UI). (4) PDF/slide import is a lossy AI text-dump, not revisable notes with visual fidelity. Slide images are never extracted (only unpdf's extractText, no rasterization), so diagrams/graphs/screenshots vanish silently, and PPTX (explicitly advertised in the same import dialog) is flat-out rejected by a server-side validation bug (kindAccepts only allows .pdf for the "slides" kind). (5) Ask-your-notes is solid (citations, notebook filter, graceful empty-corpus message); plain Search is a hard-capped 16-row Quick Switcher popup whose promised operators (tag:, notebook:, "phrase", -exclude) are completely unimplemented, so the student would reach for Ask over Search every time. (6) exam week fails the lens's explicit check: there is no way to filter the SRS queue to one notebook, anywhere client or server, despite the identical filter existing on the Ask page one click away. (7) at semester scale the notebook page (tag chips, sort, 200-note cap) holds up fine, but the dashboard's flat top-8 "Recent lessons" becomes near-useless noise once 5 modules are all active, and math/wikilinks imported months earlier stay permanently dead/unrendered. A secondary, pervasive cosmetic bug (every imported note's title is duplicated as the note's own first heading) degrades the "premium" feel on essentially every card and preview surface in the app. None of this is present in the test suite's blind spots by accident. Several of these (wikilink-as-literal-text, pptx rejection, no attachment retrieval) are one-line-of-evidence bugs that a single manual walkthrough of the real workflows would have caught immediately.

findings.title: Photo/PDF originals are captured but never shown back to the student: "never destructive OCR" is unimplemented
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/notes.ts has no GET attachments route; server/src/lib/serialize.ts never reads the attachments table; server/src/routes/imports.ts:291-296 writes the attachments row but nothing ever reads it back; web/src/lib/types.ts:104 has an `attachmentId` field that is never rendered by any component in web/src. Grep for any 'view original photo' / attachment thumbnail UI across web/src returns nothing.
findings.suggestion: Add GET /api/notes/:id/attachments (or fold into GET /api/notes/:id) and render the stored photo/PDF thumbnail pinned next to the transcription, exactly as SPEC.md §4.4 requires, so a mistranscribed word/equation/diagram can be checked against the source.

findings.title: Lecture-slides PPTX and transcript DOCX uploads are silently rejected by the server despite being advertised in the same dialog
findings.evidence: In server/src/routes/imports.ts:74-79, kindAccepts('slides', ...) only returns true for mime==='application/pdf' or ext==='.pdf' (no pptx branch); kindAccepts('transcript', ...) only allows pdf/txt/md (no docx branch). Meanwhile web/src/features/import/kinds.ts:26-40 explicitly lists 'pptx' in exts and 'PDF or PPTX' in the hint for 'slides', and 'docx' + 'PDF or DOCX' for 'transcript'. extract.ts fully implements pptx/docx parsing via officeparser, but it's just never reachable. e2e/import.spec.ts only exercises slides.pdf, never a .pptx fixture, so this shipped untested.
findings.suggestion: Fix kindAccepts to match the officeparser MIME/ext table already used elsewhere in the same file (MIME_EXT/ALLOWED_EXT), and add a real .pptx e2e fixture. This is the single most common lecture-slide format a UK CS student actually gets from Moodle.

findings.title: Phone capture (/capture) always creates a brand-new note, so using it twice for one multi-page sheet fragments the lecture
findings.evidence: In web/src/features/import/CapturePage.tsx:87-98, upload() unconditionally does `form.append('mode', 'new')`, and captureAnother() (line 121-129) only resets local UI state, never carries forward a noteId/append-mode. Contrast with web/src/features/import/ImportModal.tsx:156-184, which does exactly this chaining correctly (chainRef.current.mode = 'append' after page 1), but that's the desktop dialog, not the actual phone-capture surface the QR pairing flow sends a student to.
findings.suggestion: Give /capture the same chain-state as ImportModal: after the first successful capture, subsequent 'Capture another' uploads in the same session should default to mode='append' against the just-created note, with an explicit 'start a new note instead' escape hatch.

findings.title: PDF slide import never extracts slide images: it's a lossy AI text-dump, not the spec'd per-slide image+text block
findings.evidence: server/src/lib/extract.ts:43-53 uses only unpdf's extractText, and no getDocumentProxy/page-rasterization is ever called, despite SPEC.md §4.4 explicitly naming both APIs. server/src/routes/imports.ts:217-225 (kind==='slides') runs extract → AI restructure → save, with no image step and no literal 1:1 fallback if the AI call fails (the whole import job just fails).
findings.suggestion: Rasterize each PDF page to an image (unpdf's getDocumentProxy + canvas, or pdf-to-image) and insert one image block + one editable text block per slide as the default, non-AI, always-available path; make the AI 'restructure into an outline' pass an explicit opt-in on top of that, matching what the spec already designed.

findings.title: Wikilinks in every imported/AI-generated note render as permanently dead plain text, not links
findings.evidence: server/src/lib/markdown.ts:36-42: the `extensions` array passed to generateJSON (used by seed.ts and all three import paths) is [StarterKit, TaskList, TaskItem, TableKit, Image] and omits Wikilink, while web/src/features/editor/buildExtensions.ts:82 registers Wikilink for the live editor. server/test/markdown.test.ts:104-109 codifies this as intended ('preserves [[wikilinks]] as literal text'). Visually confirmed in docs/review-pack/04-editor-note.png / 09-editor-dark.png: '[[B-Trees and Balanced Search Trees]]' renders as unstyled plain text inside a table cell. The unlinked-mentions repair scanner (server/src/routes/notes.ts:293-308) excludes it too, since the regex-based syncLinks already created a links row, so there's no UI path to fix it short of deleting and retyping.
findings.suggestion: Register the Wikilink node (with a server-safe renderer, not the React node view) in markdown.ts's extensions list, or post-process generateJSON output to convert literal [[Title]] text runs into real wikilink nodes resolved against the notes table.

findings.title: Math notation ($...$/$$...$$) from photo-OCR, slide, and transcript imports never renders as KaTeX
findings.evidence: server/src/lib/markdown.ts:36-42 has no Mathematics extension and no LaTeX preprocessing, yet server/src/ai/prompts.ts:137 (ocrPhotoPrompt), :164 (slidesRestructurePrompt), and :183 (transcriptNotesPrompt) all explicitly instruct the model to output '$...$'/'$$...$$'. No test in server/test/markdown.test.ts exercises math at all.
findings.suggestion: Either add the Mathematics extension to the server conversion pipeline plus a preprocessing pass that turns $...$/$$...$$ into the HTML the extension's parseHTML expects before generateJSON, or explicitly downgrade the prompts to plain-text math description until that's built. Right now the prompt promises something the pipeline can't deliver, which is worse than not promising it.

findings.title: Imported notes duplicate their own title as the first line of the body, everywhere
findings.evidence: At server/src/routes/imports.ts:114-138, resolveTitle() extracts a title (AI or firstHeading(markdown)) but createNoteFromMarkdown() then converts the FULL markdown (including that same heading line) into content_json/content_text. Visually confirmed in docs/review-pack/04-editor-note.png (H1 'Big-O Notation & Complexity Analysis' page title immediately followed by an identical H1 inside the body) and in every dashboard/notebook-list/search card (01, 02, 16, 18) where the redundant title text eats into the ~160-char snippet, e.g. 'Big-O Notation & Complexity Analysis Big-O notation describes how an algorithm's...'.
findings.suggestion: Strip the exact heading resolveTitle() used (or the AI-generated title, fuzzy-matched against the first heading) from the markdown before calling markdownToTipTap, the same way a human would delete the redundant line.

findings.title: Flashcard study queue cannot be filtered to one notebook, making it impossible to cram one module before its exam
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/study.ts (whole file): /queue, /cards, /stats all operate over every flashcard regardless of notebook, and flashcardDto() never even returns a notebookId/notebookName. web/src/features/study/StudyPage.tsx:33 states 'Spaced repetition across every notebook' with no filter control anywhere in StudyPage/ReviewTab/BrowseTab. The identical need is already solved one page away. web/src/features/ask/AskPage.tsx:110-124 has working per-notebook filter chips for Ask.
findings.suggestion: Add notebookId to the flashcard/note join, expose it in flashcardDto, and add the same filter-chip row Ask AI already has to both ReviewTab and BrowseTab. This is explicitly called out as a make-or-break exam-week check.

findings.title: Search operators ("phrase", -exclude, tag:, notebook:) are completely unimplemented despite being spec'd and implied by the UI's own planned empty state
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/search.ts:17-25 (sanitizeFtsQuery) strips quotes entirely and strips all punctuation from every token via `t.replace(/[^\p{L}\p{N}_'-]+/gu, '')`, then blind-ANDs every remaining word. There is no operator-recognition logic anywhere in the function or the route. A query like `notebook:Databases joins` silently becomes a search for the literal words "notebook", "databases", and "joins". No client-side code parses these operators either (grep across web/src for 'notebook:' / 'tag:#' finds nothing relevant).
findings.suggestion: Implement the thin query-parser SPEC.md §4.3 describes: strip recognized `tag:`/`notebook:`/`-word`/`"phrase"` tokens into real SQL WHERE clauses before building the FTS MATCH string, and surface which operators are supported in the empty-results state as originally planned.

findings.title: Mobile dashboard overflows horizontally on a real phone viewport: cards cut off mid-sentence with no scroll affordance
findings.evidence: docs/review-pack/16-dashboard-mobile.png, captured with Playwright's real 'Pixel 7' device profile (412 CSS px wide) per scripts/review-pack.mjs:415: the hero card and the 'Recent lessons' note-grid both render two card columns side by side and get hard-clipped at the viewport edge. web/src/styles/pages.css:113-117 sizes `.note-grid` with `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(230px, 1fr))`, which needs ≥472px for two tracks but the available mobile content width is ~380px (412 - 32px padding). It should collapse to one column and isn't.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit single-column override for `.note-grid`/`.dash__hero` under the existing max-width:520px media query rather than relying on auto-fill's implicit sizing, and re-run the review-pack mobile shots to confirm the overflow is gone.

findings.title: No per-notebook "last time in X" recall: the dashboard's promised 5-minutes-before-lecture check fails
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/dashboard.ts:7-66 has no per-notebook recent-note query; the 'notebooks' array it returns (lines 44-56) only carries noteCount + lastNoteAt, no note title/snippet. web/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx:102-134 shows a single global `continueNote` (whatever was edited most recently across ALL notebooks) plus a flat, ungrouped `recent` grid. There is no mini self-test from cue/definition spans either, as SPEC.md §4.2 requires.
findings.suggestion: Add a per-notebook 'last session note' query (MAX(updated_at) per notebook_id, joined back to the note) and render it as a small recall row per notebook near the top of the dashboard, so finding last Tuesday's Databases lecture note really is one glance, not a scan-and-guess through a mixed feed.

findings.title: The same note is shown twice, back-to-back, on the dashboard
findings.evidence: server/src/routes/dashboard.ts:8-10 sets `continueRow = recentRows[0]`, and line 58-61 returns `recent: recentRows.map(noteLite)` which still includes index 0, so the same note backs both the hero 'Continue where you left off' card and the first card of the 'Recent lessons' grid directly beneath it. Visually confirmed in docs/review-pack/01-dashboard-light.png (both cards read 'Flat Hunting Notes', '3h ago').
findings.suggestion: Exclude recentRows[0] (or whichever note becomes continueNote) from the `recent` array before sending it to the client.

findings.title: PDF slide import has no literal fallback if the AI restructure call fails: the whole import is lost even though text was already extracted
findings.evidence: At server/src/routes/imports.ts:217-225, kind==='slides' always calls chat(slidesRestructurePrompt(...)) with no branch that saves the raw per-page text if every model in the fallback list fails; the catch block at line 251-257 just marks the job 'failed', discarding the already-successful extraction.
findings.suggestion: On AI failure for slides, fall back to a literal one-heading-per-slide note built directly from the already-extracted page text, matching the fallback design SPEC.md §4.4 explicitly calls for.

findings.title: AI-suggested cue questions after photo transcription (should-tier, named in SPEC.md's own prompt example) were never implemented
findings.evidence: server/src/ai/prompts.ts:126-148: the shipped ocrPhotoPrompt() rules list has no instruction for the 'trailing JSON block' of 3-5 candidate cue questions that SPEC.md §6 describes as part of this exact prompt.
findings.suggestion: Add the cue-question instruction back to ocrPhotoPrompt, parse the trailing block in imports.ts, and surface it as a tick-to-keep review panel after transcription completes, as spec'd.

findings.title: "Extract action items → to-do blocks" AI feature is missing from the note AI menu
findings.evidence: In web/src/features/editor/NotePage.tsx:392-424, the AI dropdown only offers Improve writing, Summarize, Generate flashcards, and Suggest title; there is no action-item extraction anywhere in the client or an /api/ai/action-items route.
findings.suggestion: If deprioritized intentionally, remove it from BACKLOG.md's must-fix list; otherwise implement it alongside the existing summarize/flashcards AI actions, which already have the accept/reject plumbing this would reuse.

findings.title: Comments/margin-notes feature has a full DB schema but zero client implementation
findings.evidence: server/src/schema.sql defines note_comments with resolved/unresolved state, and SPEC.md §4.8/§7 documents the full API contract, but grep across web/src for any comment-rendering component returns nothing relevant.
findings.suggestion: Either build the mark-based comment UI (bubble menu entry + margin popover thread) or drop it from BACKLOG.md's should-tier list so the gap isn't silently carried forward.

findings.title: No exam/topic coverage tracker or local graph view, so study is pure card-by-card SRS with no per-module progress sense
findings.evidence: web/src/main.tsx:13-26 has no /graph route and no coverage-tracker page; web/src/features/study/* has no topic/coverage UI beyond the raw due-count and 14-day heatmap already on the dashboard.
findings.suggestion: If exam-week motivation matters as much as the lens implies, a simple per-notebook 3-state topic self-rating table (student-entered rows, linked flashcard due-count) would directly answer 'is the review loop motivating' better than a bare due-count.

findings.title: Search has no dedicated results page: it's a hard-capped 16-row Quick Switcher popup
findings.evidence: web/src/main.tsx has no /search route; web/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx:61 calls api.searchTitles(q, 8) and api.search(q, 8): 8 title results + 8 full-text results, hard-capped, with no 'see more' and no way to combine with a notebook/tag filter from within the popup itself.
findings.suggestion: Either promote the full-text section of the Quick Switcher into a real /search page with pagination and the (once-implemented) operator support, or explicitly scope the Quick Switcher's job to titles-only per the spec's original intent and give full-text search its own surface.
